title: Iran executes two men convicted of spying for Israel's Mossad intelligence agency
summary: Iran hanged Yaghoub Karimpour and Nasser Bekrzadeh after the Supreme Court upheld death sentences for espionage, including transmitting information about Natanz nuclear facility to Israel.
contents:
## Iran Executes Two Convicted Spies

### Executions Confirmed
Iran announced on May 2, 2026, the execution by hanging of two men convicted of spying for Israel. The Iranian judiciary's news outlet Mizanonline confirmed Yaghoub Karimpour and Nasser Bekrzadeh faced execution after the Supreme Court upheld their death sentences.

### Espionage Charges
Karimpour faced accusations of sending "sensitive information" to an officer in Israel's Mossad intelligence agency. Bekrzadeh allegedly transmitted details about government and religious leaders, along with information about Natanz — home to a nuclear enrichment facility bombed by Israel and the US during the current conflict.

### Pattern of Executions
Iran has hanged more than a dozen people over alleged espionage and terrorist activities in recent weeks. Human rights groups note that Iran routinely conducts closed-door trials in which defendants cannot challenge accusations against them.

### Wartime Context
The executions occurred during the 64th day of the US-Israel war against Iran, amid a fragile ceasefire. The intensified crackdown on suspected espionage reflects heightened security concerns following strikes on nuclear and military infrastructure.
